seoTitle: Hayes Gap vs Haymarket — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hayes Gap and Haymarket. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hayes Gap vs Haymarket: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hayes Gap and Haymarket.
heading: Hayes Gap vs Haymarket — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hayes Gap, New South Wales and Haymarket, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hayes Gap has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Haymarket (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hayes Gap or Haymarket safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hayes Gap has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Haymarket scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hayes Gap scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Haymarket scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hayes Gap or Haymarket?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hayes Gap has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
